items.trigger: A user choice would change scope, data ownership, output shape, destructive behavior, public behavior, or long-lived state.
items.input: The pending decision, available options, current task context, stakes, and known constraints.
steps: Name what is being decided in plain language.
State the consequence of a wrong choice.
Recommend one option with a reason.
Compare options by completeness, benefit, cost, and risk.
Stop for user choice unless the role is an execution-only child agent with an explicit default.
items.output: A concise decision brief or a blocked status when no human can answer.
items.boundary: Do not use for trivial formatting choices or already explicit instructions.
items.decision_reason: All six fields are present and the workflow is repeatable across high-impact decisions.

items.candidate: Cleaned-body extraction for engineering Skill materials.
items.trigger: A source mixes reader-relevant workflow content with install commands, environment checks, sync, telemetry, or debug protocol.
items.input: Full selected Skill or engineering source plus material-type contract.
steps: Identify purpose, triggers, user intent, workflow, constraints, failure conditions, output requirements, and design highlights.
Move commands, paths, machine state, repeated templates, and runtime protocol out of the body.
Record removed categories in audit or package README.
Write body prose that preserves the material's operational logic without turning it into a summary.
items.output: A cleaned body page plus audit notes for removed categories.
items.boundary: Do not use when the reader's primary goal is executing the command reference itself.
evidence: ReaderLab demo contract for Skill / engineering material
Preamble and runtime shell sections in source
Cleaned body produced in this demo
items.decision_reason: Meets trigger, input, steps, output, boundary, and evidence requirements.

items.candidate: Create a formal reusable Skill for all decision prompts.
evidence: Candidate was downgraded in tournament because independent scope is not yet proven.
items.decision_reason: The idea is useful, but it lacks a complete standalone input contract, boundaries, and independent evidence for formal Skill creation.

items.candidate: Evidence-first technical interrogation for spec writing.
items.trigger: A spec or issue request touches an existing codebase, API, data model, UI, infrastructure, or tests.
items.input: User request plus at least one verified project artifact such as file path, package manifest, directory structure, existing doc, or search result.
steps: Identify the likely project area from the request.
Read or search the smallest relevant evidence before asking technical questions.
Cite the evidence when asking unresolved questions.
Skip questions already answered by the evidence.
Record remaining uncertainty in the draft spec.
items.output: A technical interrogation section grounded in current project evidence.
items.boundary: Do not use this to justify broad repo scans or reading old unrelated reports.
evidence: Issue Quality Standards: Verified Current State
items.decision_reason: All six fields are present and the operation is repeatable for spec workflows touching existing systems.

items.candidate: Adopt gstack runtime shell as ReaderLab default.
items.boundary: Rejected because it would install or normalize foreign runtime behavior outside this demo scope.
evidence: User explicitly prohibited installing, syncing, enabling, or writing formal Skill assets.
items.decision_reason: Missing required Skill fields and conflicts with the demo boundary.
